message.text:
i would like to make a connect 4 game in html, css, and javascript. the game should meet the following requirements:
6 by 7 grid
AI opponent (red)
player controls (yellow)
alternating turns
on win, player can select "restart"
win happens when any 4 in a row occurs
draw occurs if board is full and no winner
the player can move by clicking on the column they want to drop their circle in, or by hitting the number 1-7

for now, the AI opponent can just select a free column at random. if the player tries to play in a full column, nothing should happen. the player should go first. the board should be a 6 row, 7 column grid with white circles in blank spots, red or yellow when disks are there, and a blue grid where there are not circles.

NEVER just mention or reference a skill in your response without actually reading it first. If a skill is relevant, load it before proceeding.

3. When multiple skills apply (e.g., a flowchart in documentation), load all relevant skills

response.value: I’ll scaffold a complete browser-based Connect 4 game now and start by checking the workspace contents so I can create the needed files in the right place.
I found an empty repository, so I’m creating the core files next: one HTML file, one CSS file, and one JavaScript file with full game logic and random AI turns.

edits.text: You are Yellow. Red is the AI.
Click a numbered column button or press keys 1-7 to drop a yellow disk.
function checkDirection(row, col, rowStep, colStep, player) {
if (r >= 0 && r < ROWS && c >= 0 && c < COLS && board[r][c] === player) {
setGameOver("Red wins. Press Restart to play again.");
// If the selected column is full, ignore the move.
setGameOver("Yellow wins. Press Restart to play again.");

response.value: The game files are in place; I’m running an error check now to catch any syntax issues before I hand it over.

Implemented a complete Connect 4 web game that matches your requirements in:

-

What is included:

- 6 by 7 board
- Player is Yellow and goes first
- AI is Red and picks a random available column
- Alternating turns between player and AI
- Win detection for any 4 in a row
- Draw detection when the board is full with no winner
- Restart button after win/draw (and available anytime)
- Player input by:
1. Clicking column buttons 1-7
2. Pressing keyboard keys 1-7
- Full-column moves are ignored exactly as requested
- Visual style:
1. Blue board grid
2. White empty circles
3. Yellow and red filled circles for pieces

Validation status:

- I ran workspace error checking and there are no reported errors.

If you want, next I can add simple drop animations for pieces while keeping all current behavior unchanged.

slashCommands.name: compact
slashCommands.description: Free up context by compacting the conversation history. Optionally include extra instructions for compaction.

slashCommands.name: explain
slashCommands.description: Explain how the code in your active editor works

slashCommands.name: tests
slashCommands.description: Generate unit tests for the selected code

disambiguation.description: The user wants to generate unit tests.
examples: Generate tests for my selection using pytest.

slashCommands.name: fix
slashCommands.description: Propose a fix for the problems in the selected code
slashCommands.sampleRequest: There is a problem in this code. Rewrite the code to show it with the bug fixed.

slashCommands.name: new
slashCommands.description: Scaffold code for a new file or project in a workspace
slashCommands.sampleRequest: Create a RESTful API server using typescript

disambiguation.description: The user wants to create a complete Visual Studio Code workspace from scratch, such as a new application or a Visual Studio Code extension. Use this category only if the question relates to generating or creating new workspaces in Visual Studio Code. Do not use this category for updating existing code or generating sample code snippets
examples: Create a sample project which uses the fileSystemProvider API.

slashCommands.name: newNotebook
slashCommands.sampleRequest: How do I create a notebook to load data from a csv file?

disambiguation.description: The user wants to create a new Jupyter notebook in Visual Studio Code.
examples: Create a notebook to analyze this CSV file.

slashCommands.name: setupTests
slashCommands.description: Set up tests in your project (Experimental)
slashCommands.sampleRequest: add playwright tests to my project

disambiguation.description: The user wants to configure project test setup, framework, or test runner. The user does not want to fix their existing tests.
examples: Set up tests for this project.

response.value: I’m updating the game modes and controls now by inspecting the current HTML/CSS/JS, then I’ll replace the restart flow with four bottom mode buttons and implement the new AI behaviors.

I’ve confirmed the current structure and I’m now editing all three files: removing restart, adding four mode buttons at the bottom, and refactoring game logic to support 2-player, easy AI, hard AI, and 2-AI hard-vs-hard play.

edits.text:
Connect 4

Choose a mode below. Yellow always starts.

Yellow turn

Click a numbered column button or press keys 1-7.

response.value: The mode system and AI logic are implemented; next I’m running a quick error pass to ensure everything is clean and then I’ll summarize exactly how each mode behaves.

Updated exactly as requested.

What changed:
1. Removed the restart button entirely.
2. Added 4 mode buttons at the bottom:
- 2 player
- easy
- hard
- 2 AI
3. Kept yellow as the starting player for all modes.
4. Kept click and keyboard 1-7 input for human moves.
5. Full-column human moves are ignored (no action).

Mode behavior now:
1. 2 player: both Yellow and Red are human-controlled.
2. easy: Red AI picks a random valid column.
3. hard: Red AI picks in this order:
- winning move
- block opponent winning move
- random valid move
4. 2 AI: Yellow and Red both use the same hard AI and play automatically.

Files updated:
-

Notes:
- Game-over messages now instruct the player to choose a mode button to restart a new game.
- Syntax/error check reports clean for all three files.
